feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Combining apps from different Work Zone Content Providers/Packages in one CDM
entry.content:
--- Please ignore this question  --- See here  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/combining-apps-cards-from-multiple-content-providers-packages-in-one-cdm/qaq-p/14308986 ---

Dear experts in this space and dear broader SAP community, 

I am trying to combine apps from different Work Zone Content Providers/Packages in one CDM (Common Data Model) definition of another Content Provider. Example - One Content Package/Provider is providing some cards/apps to my Work Zone tenant, and I would like to consume these cards/apps in a Work Space CDM definition of another Content Provider. 

Similar things can be done using the Work Zone Content Manager allowing to combine apps/cards from different Content Providers/Packages into one Work Page. Nevertheless, I couldn't figure out how to do this within a CDM definition. Prefixing the app ID with the other Content Provider ID (see below) did not do the job (e.g., content.provider_app.id). Any help or input is highly appreciated. 

My goal is to have a CDM, that contains a Work Page, that references apps from multiple other Content Packages/Providers. 

@matthias_schmalz @gregorw @jmargieh - Maybe you have already done something similar?

Thanks in advance and all the best, 
Martin

{
    "_version": "3.2.0",
    "identification": {
      "id": "combined-workpage",
      "entityType": "workpage",
      "title": "{{Title}}"
    },
    "payload": {
      "workpageConfig": {
        "title": "Combined Work Page"
      },
      "rows": [
        {
          "id": "row-1",
          "rowConfig": {},
          "columns": [
            {
              "id": "col-11",
              "columnConfig": {},
              "cells": [
                {
                  "id": "cell-11",
                  "cellConfig": {},
                  "widgets": [
                    {
                      "id": "widg-11",
                      "viz": {
                        "appId": "other.package_cards.forecast.app",
                        "vizId": "other.package_cards.forecast.viz" 
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "id": "widg-12",
                      "viz": {
                        "appId": "applocalprovider",
                        "vizId": "applocalprovider-display"
                      }
                    }
                  ]
                }
              ]
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    "texts": [
      {
        "locale": "",
        "textDictionary": {
          "Title": "User Page"
        }
      }
    ]
  },

entry.title: Combining apps/cards from multiple Content Providers/Packages in one CDM (Common Data Model)
entry.content:
Dear experts in this space and dear broader SAP community, 

I am trying to combine apps from different Work Zone Content Providers/Packages in one CDM (Common Data Model) definition of another Content Provider. Example - One Content Package/Provider is providing some cards/apps to my Work Zone tenant, and I would like to consume these cards/apps in a Work Space CDM definition of another Content Provider. 

Similar things can be done using the Work Zone Content Manager allowing to combine apps/cards from different Content Providers/Packages into one Work Page. Nevertheless, I couldn't figure out how to do this within a CDM definition. Prefixing the app ID with the other Content Provider ID (see below) did not do the job (e.g., content.provider_app.id). Any help or input is highly appreciated. 

My goal is to have a CDM, that contains a Work Page, that references apps from multiple other Content Packages/Providers. 

@matthias_schmalz @gregorw @jmargieh - Maybe you have already done something similar?

Thanks in advance and all the best, 
Martin

 

{
    "_version": "3.2.0",
    "identification": {
      "id": "combined-workpage",
      "entityType": "workpage",
      "title": "{{Title}}"
    },
    "payload": {
      "workpageConfig": {
        "title": "Combined Work Page"
      },
      "rows": [
        {
          "id": "row-1",
          "rowConfig": {},
          "columns": [
            {
              "id": "col-11",
              "columnConfig": {},
              "cells": [
                {
                  "id": "cell-11",
                  "cellConfig": {},
                  "widgets": [
                    {
                      "id": "widg-11",
                      "viz": {
                        "appId": "other.package_cards.forecast.app",
                        "vizId": "other.package_cards.forecast.viz" 
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "id": "widg-12",
                      "viz": {
                        "appId": "applocalprovider",
                        "vizId": "applocalprovider-display"
                      }
                    }
                  ]
                }
              ]
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    "texts": [
      {
        "locale": "",
        "textDictionary": {
          "Title": "User Page"
        }
      }
    ]
  },

entry.title: How can I use Google Tag manager in Workzone?
entry.content:
I want to track outbound clicks from my Workzone site since it's acting as a front-end to a SuccessFactors app that supports business processes. I want to compare how many users are using the guidance on workzone to complete SF tasks.

I've had Google analytics on the site since launch using the 3rd party analytics option in the workzone admin console. Great. 

Google Tag Manager is the best practice approach to tracking outbound clicks, but there's no way to add the GTM container snippet to workzone which is necessary for this.

How can I use GTM in Workzone?

GA's native outbound link tracking (i.e. not using GTM) would have been good enough had the GA event report returned link_text. It doesn't, although it should according to spec, probably because of the complex messy html that workzone writes.

entry.title: How to use same host name when accessing app in new tab using Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and created a local app to access the sap on premise system application using Dynamic URL as below:

When using on premise app of ABAP system it is using the same host of work zone when opening in same window.

however when opening the app in new window it is changing the host name and adding the word "sapdelim" and run  time destination in the url:

Please let me know how we can use the same host name (without Run Time destination) as work zone when opening in new window.


Regards,

Rohit Gera

entry.title: Principal Propagation via SAP Build Work Zone for S/4HANA content not working.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

 

we configured principal propagation and exposed our S/4 content as described on the following blogs:

  • Exposing Content from SAP S/4HANA
  • 3452851 - Einrichten der Weitergabe von Benutzeridentitäten schrittweise über SAP Cloud Connector - SAP for Me
  • Integration Guide: SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

It worked fine when we used basic auth in Runtime Destination. When switching to principal propagation it looks like this:

 

Console log:

Heres our Design time

Runtime

 

We also updated the channel manager in work zone after editing the destinations.

SCC Log doesnt show anything suspicious. 
Greetings
Rouven

entry.title: Is SAP Build subscription alone sufficient to create mobile applications, or is SAP Build Apps subsc
entry.content:
As per SAP documentation, SAP Build is a bundle that includes the following services:

  1. SAP Build Apps

  2. SAP Build Process Automation

  3. SAP Build Work Zone

I would like to clarify the licensing and subscription aspect.

If we subscribe only to SAP Build, is that sufficient to create and deploy mobile applications using SAP Build Apps?

Or do we need to purchase/subscribe to SAP Build Apps separately, even though it is part of the SAP Build bundle?

It would be helpful if someone could explain:

Whether SAP Build Apps is automatically included with SAP Build

Or if it requires an individual subscription/entitlement

Any limitations (users, runtime, mobile deployment) with SAP Build Apps under SAP Build

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: Does anyone faced the SAP UI overlapping issue in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with UI overlapping in the Fiori launchpad build with SAP Build workzone. When user is trying to navigate between the applications within Fiori launchpad the screen is splitting into two and 2 apps are being displayed as overlapping. Need any experts help here to understand the issue and rectification.

entry.title: Build Work Zone Returning 401
entry.content:
I have deployed an app on SAP BTP. The app has a service worker as it is a PWA and served through Build Work Zone Managed App Router. Everything works fine until the authentication cookie expires. Once the cookie expires, the app triggers reloading the index.html page, detects that the user is not logged in anymore and takes us to Build Work Zone login screen. In the authentication process, https://*.ondemand.com/login/callback?authType=ias&code
always gets 401 . If I remove the service worker , it works . Even if the service worker does not intercept any request, just it's presence is breaking the auth flow. 
Is there anyone who can help me figure out what's wrong here. Thanks for your time and suggestion.

entry.title: Fiori Migration to SAP BTP: Best Practice for Multi-Backend (Region-Based) Routing Without SAP Gatew
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am working on a Fiori migration project and would like guidance on the recommended architecture for handling multiple backend systems by region after moving to SAP BTP.

Current  Architecture

  • An on-premise SAP Gateway hosts a set of Fiori apps.

  • The Gateway forwards OData calls to multiple backend systems (ECC) based on system alias.

  • This setup allows region-based routing, but currently all backends are technically reachable, and multiple backends may be called during app execution.

Target Architecture

  • Decommission the on-premise SAP Gateway system.

  • Host Fiori apps on SAP BTP and expose them via SAP Build Work Zone.

  • Keep the same backend ECC systems (one per region).

  • No central Gateway system remains for system-alias-based routing.

Our Proposed Approach

  • Define one SAP BTP Destination per backend system (e.g. one per region).

  • Use the same destination names across subaccounts (DEV / QAS / PRD), with destination details varying per subaccount.

  • Configure the Fiori app to call all destinations and aggregate the results on the UI side.

     

Questions

  1. Is this approach (calling all backends and aggregating results in the UI) considered a good practice on SAP BTP?

  2. Are there recommended alternatives for region-based backend routing without a central SAP Gateway?

  3. How is user-region–based backend selection typically handled in BTP-centric Fiori architectures?

Any reference architectures or SAP-recommended patterns would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: Clarification on SAP Build Apps Subscription Visibility under build-default Plan
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We need your clarification regarding SAP Build Apps subscription visibility in our SAP BTP subaccount.

Currently, our subaccount has the following subscriptions active under the build-default plan:

SAP Build Process Automation – build-default

SAP Business Application Studio – build-default

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – build-default

As per SAP documentation and licensing understanding, SAP Build Apps is included within the build-default plan and does not appear as a standalone subscription.

However, when we attempt to create a project using Create Project → Web Application (low-code) in SAP Build Apps, the system displays the status as “Not Subscribed”, which creates confusion from an entitlement perspective.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether SAP Build Apps entitlement is correctly provided through the build-default plan in our subaccount, and

  2. Why the project creation wizard displays “Not Subscribed” despite the build-default plan being active.

Your confirmation will help us proceed and also communicate the correct information to our stakeholders.

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: MyInbox App in Work Build Zone only scrolls to currently selected item
entry.content:
The following behavior is currently persistent in our MyInbox in the Build Work Zone:

- We have selected an item from the list which is either a) not at the top of the list or b) will not be at the top of the list after a refresh.

After pressing the refresh button, the "All tasks" bar doesn't slide to the top to showcase the latest document that has come in or may be hidden there - this is indicated in the screenshot below. So there may always be any items at the top of the list which we don't see.

We are calling this in our code by 

this.getInboxAPI()?.updateTaskList();

This is internally somehow delegated to 

 

this.getOwnerComponent().getEventBus().publish("com.sap.spa.inbox", "refreshListInternal")

The question now is, do we have any further control over this?

entry.title: Widget to show a feed containing more than 15 items
entry.content:
I'm using a Workzone content widget to display blog posts contained in a specified content folder. Great.

The max number of items it can display is 15. This means posts that drop out of the top 15 simply aren't displayed and they're lost to users. The widget doesn't have paging controls, which seems like a bit of an oversight. How are colleagues supposed to find older articles?

I've had a look through the other widgets and I can't find anything that will serve as a long-term space for blog type articles. 

What's the solution here?

entry.title: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error on OData $metadata in SAP Build Work Zone (BTP ABAP Trial)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am deploying a SAPUI5 application to SAP Build Work Zone using the Managed AppRouter, connected to the BTP ABAP Trial environment.

The app works perfectly locally in BAS, but in Work Zone, it fails to load data. The browser console shows an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error when fetching the OData $metadata and Fiori annotations.

Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553100 [ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed
Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553500 Error: HTTP request failed

My Configuration:

  • Destination has HTML5.DynamicDestination = true and WebIDEEnabled = true.

  • Destination has HTML5.ForwardAuthToken = true.

  • My xs-app.json route uses "authenticationType": "xsuaa".

  • I have already cleared the Work Zone site cache.

I have attached my manifest.json, routing file, and a screenshot of my Destination configuration for reference. Any help is greatly appreciated!

manifest.json

{
  "_version": "1.80.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "zcpequipmentapp",
    "type": "application",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:lrop",
      "version": "1.20.2",
      "toolsId": "4c2d36ce-a572-4ef5-a4cb-4bd72a531d54"
    },
    "crossNavigation": {
      "inbounds": {
        "Equipment-manage": {
          "semanticObject": "Equipment",
          "action": "manage",
          "title": "{{appTitle}}",
          "subTitle": "{{appDescription}}",
          "icon": "sap-icon://machine",
          "signature": {
            "parameters": {},
            "additionalParameters": "allowed"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "dataSources": {
      "ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN',Version='0001')/$value/",
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN.xml"
        }
      },
      "annotation": {
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
        }
      },
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCP_SB_EQUIP/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": ["ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN", "annotation"],
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
          "odataVersion": "2.0"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "sap-icon://machine",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.142.6",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {},
        "sap.ushell": {},
        "sap.f": {},
        "sap.ui.comp": {},
        "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
        "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "zcpequipmentapp.i18n.i18n"
        }
      },
      "": {
        "dataSource": "mainService",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
          "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
          "refreshAfterChange": false,
          "metadataUrlParams": {
            "sap-value-list": "none"
          }
        }
      },
      "@i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    "services": {
      "ShellUIService": {
        "factoryName": "sap.ushell.ui5service.ShellUIService"
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": []
    },
    "routing": {
      "config": {},
      "routes": [],
      "targets": {}
    }
  },
  "sap.ui.generic.app": {
    "_version": "1.3.0",
    "settings": {
      "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
      "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
      "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
      "showDraftToggle": false
    },
    "pages": {
      "ListReport|Equipment": {
        "entitySet": "Equipment",
        "component": {
          "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport",
          "list": true,
          "settings": {
            "condensedTableLayout": true,
            "smartVariantManagement": true,
            "enableTableFilterInPageVariant": true,
            "filterSettings": {
              "dateSettings": {
                "useDateRange": true
              }
            },
            "tableSettings": {
              "type": "ResponsiveTable"
            }
          }
        },
        "pages": {
          "ObjectPage|Equipment": {
            "entitySet": "Equipment",
            "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
            "component": {
              "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.fiori": {
    "registrationIds": ["F1234"],
    "archeType": "transactional"
  },
  "sap.cloud": {
    "public": true,
    "service": "zcpequipmentapp"
  }
}


mta.yaml

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: zcpequipmentapp
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
        parameters:
          content-target: true
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
    parameters:
      content:
        instance:
          destinations:
            - Name: zcpequipmentapp_html_repo_host
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
            - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              Name: zcpequipmentapp_uaa
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
          existing_destinations_policy: update
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true

  - name: zcpequipmentapp-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - zcpequipmentapp.zip
          name: zcpequipmentapp
          target-path: resources/

  - name: zcpequipmentapp
    type: html5
    path: .
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
      service-plan: application
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
      service-plan: app-host

parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true


destination

entry.title: User is unable to login to SAP Build Work Zone due to "Subaccount reached its limit" message
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm having an issue where our developer team is unable to login to the SAP build work zone free version. The error they are getting is - " Subaccount reached its limit. Unfortunately, we couldn't log you in because your subaccount has reached the maximum limit of a free plan. A free plan is restricted to 20 named users and 2 admin users per month per subaccount ".

My doubts are as below regarding this: -

1. Can we restrict this admin access on role collections level from security side? Please advise how this can be done

2. Can this error be reset within the day? Cause our developer team needs to check an issue.

 

Regards,

Arpit

SAP Security

entry.title: Technical Issue: Authentication Popup in SAP Build Work Zone (Destination: abap-cloud-default)
entry.content:
I am currently encountering a persistent "Sign in" authentication prompt while accessing the "Simple App" within the SAP Build Work Zone (Trial environment).

Despite having the destination abap-cloud-default_abap-trial-fae783bdtrial-dev configured with OAuth2UserTokenExchange, the application is requesting manual credentials instead of passing the SSO token.

Technical Details:

  • Destination Name: abap-cloud-default_abap-trial-fae783bdtrial-dev

  • Authentication Type: OAuth2UserTokenExchange

  • Error Manifestation: Pop-up requesting Username/Password for the ABAP instance URL.

 Has anyone encountered this or found a solution? I would appreciate any insights. Thank you in advance!








_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: sample
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
- name: sample-destination-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  requires:
  - name: sample-destination-service
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  - name: sample-repo-host
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: sample-repo-host-key
  - name: sample-uaa
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: sample-uaa-key
  parameters:
    content:
      instance:
        destinations:
        - Name: sample_html_repo_host
          ServiceInstanceName: sample-html5-service
          ServiceKeyName: sample-repo-host-key
          sap.cloud.service: sample
        - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
          Name: sample_uaa
          ServiceInstanceName: sample-xsuaa-service
          ServiceKeyName: sample-uaa-key
          sap.cloud.service: sample
        existing_destinations_policy: update
  build-parameters:
    no-source: true
- name: sample-app-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: sample-repo-host
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: resources
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - sample.zip
      name: sample
      target-path: resources/
- name: sample
  type: html5
  path: .
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: sample-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: sample-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
- name: sample-uaa
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-name: sample-xsuaa-service
    service-plan: application
- name: sample-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-name: sample-html5-service
    service-plan: app-host
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true

entry.title: Error subscribing SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Cloud Identity Services in trial subacc.
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently trying to follow the course "Develop extensions with CAP following the SAP BTP Developer's Guide". 

When following the guide I have to add the service plan "SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition free (Application)" and I have to subscribe to it via Services → Service Marketplace. 

However the creation fails and returns the following error

"You haven't configured your subaccount (for business users) to trust a tenant of SAP Cloud Identity Services using OpenID Connect. SAML trust isn't supported for this service. To subscribe this application, use the Establish Trust option to configure trust between your subaccount and SAP Cloud Identity Services.
For more information, see https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/establish-trust-and-federation-between-uaa-and-identity-authentication."

I have created a standard SAP account and I am logged in via the Default identity provider

I also don't have any other tenants to configure.

To resolve this issue I then tried to also subscribe to Cloud Identity Services. This unfortunately also doesn't work and returns the following error message "Cloud Identity Services konnte nicht abonniert werden. Bitte versuchen Sie es erneut. Falls das Problem weiterhin besteht, stellen Sie eine Frage in der SAP Community."

Now I am stuck and can't further proceed with the course. I have also tried it with a completely different SAP account and trial subaccount. The same issues arise.

Is this issue already known and is there any way to resolve this?

Kind regards

entry.title: Error subscribing SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Cloud Identity Services in trial subacc.
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently trying to follow the course "Develop extensions with CAP following the SAP BTP Developer's Guide". 

When following the guide I have to add the service plan "SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition free (Application)" and I have to subscribe to it via Services → Service Marketplace. 

However the creation fails and returns the following error

"You haven't configured your subaccount (for business users) to trust a tenant of SAP Cloud Identity Services using OpenID Connect. SAML trust isn't supported for this service. To subscribe this application, use the Establish Trust option to configure trust between your subaccount and SAP Cloud Identity Services.
For more information, see https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/establish-trust-and-federation-betwee...

I have created a standard SAP account and I am logged in via the Default identity provider

 

I also don't have any other tenants to configure.

 

To resolve this issue I then tried to also subscribe to Cloud Identity Services. This unfortunately also doesn't work and returns the following error message "Cloud Identity Services konnte nicht abonniert werden. Bitte versuchen Sie es erneut. Falls das Problem weiterhin besteht, stellen Sie eine Frage in der SAP Community."

 

Now I am stuck and can't further proceed with the course. I have also tried it with a completely different SAP account and trial subaccount. The same issues arise.

Is this issue already known and is there any way to resolve this?

Kind regards

entry.title: I have no subscription option for Workzone
entry.content:
Hello,

I can only create an instance for SAP Build Workzone in my subaccount (not trial), but there is no subscription option:


I have all entitles for Workzone in my global account:

I have also subscribed to the Cloud Identity Service, but in the Trust Configuration, I have a different tenant for the Cloud Identity Service than the one in my subscriptions. Could this be the source of my problem with the missing Workzone subscription option?

Cloud Identity Service-Tenant in my Thrust:

Cloud-Identity-Service-Tenant in "Instances and Subscriptions":

I would be thankful if somebody can help me.

Best regards
Timur

entry.title: Implementing a Public/Anonymous SAPUI5 Registration App on BTP with Backend Data Validation
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

I am working on a registration application using SAPUI5 on SAP BTP. I have a prototype ready, but I need guidance on two specific technical issues:

  1. Deployment for public access: How can I deploy this registration app so it is publicly available for anyone to use without requiring a login or IAS authentication? I need the app to be accessible to external users who do not have system credentials.
  2. Accessing system data for verification: How can I securely access our backend system data from this public application? The goal is to verify if a customer already exists in our system (e.g., checking by Email or BP) before they register. Since the user is anonymous, what is the best way to handle the connection to the backend?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Why has cut-and-paste behaviour changed? [Rant]
entry.content:
So we know cutting-and-pasting can be a bit of a nightmare in Workzone because by default ctrl-v pastes in any formatting from the source material. This means that unless your editors are well drilled you get inconsistent formatting across your pages that need reformatting. 

But I'm sure many of us knew that ctrl-shift-v pastes without source formatting, so this is a simple workaround that's as easy to use as ctrl-v.

But NOW, following this week's (again unnannouced) release we're seeing a new paste behaviour: When pasting formatted text we're invited to choose from 

  1. keep source formatting
  2. clear formatting
  3. match destination formatting. 

So as a user I now have to select one of these options (none of which dismissed the modal), and if I select 1 or 2 (or just ctrl-shift-v), the content is placed on a new line.

I've counted the interactions. What was previously 1 interaction (ctrl-shift-v) is now somewhere between 3 and 10 interactions depending on your choices and how hard it is to re-combine your sentence. Plus you HAVE to move your hand from keyboard to mouse because you can't dismiss the modal with escape, adding friction to the whole experience

Well done SAP for breaking something as foundational as cutting and pasting. <slow clap>
